items.title: Former Cuban president Raul Castro indicted on murder charges in the US

children.content: The move is the latest escalation in Washington's pressure campaign against the island's communist government.

items.title: Labor burns political capital as tax reform backlash grows

items.title: Pressure builds on new homes and inflation as costs go through the roof

items.title: With El Niño waiting in the wings what's in store for Australia's winter season?

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is forecasting winter to be unusually warm across the entire country as an El Niño climate pattern is increasingly likely to take hold.

image.altText: A map of Australia showing a dry south, wetter north

items.title: Trump's pick for ambassador to Australia names three priorities for role

image.altText: David Brat sits at a desk and speaks.

items.title: 'We're not fleeing': The disappearing Pacific nation relocating its people to Australia

children.content: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.

A drone shot of Tuvalu's only international runway surrounded by ocean.

Officials were surprised when the majority of Tuvalu's population applied for the Falepili mobility scheme.

items.title: WHO-linked report warns world more at risk of pandemic now than before COVID

children.content: As health leaders face deadly outbreaks of Ebola and hantavirus, a major report finds the world's response system remains dangerously underprepared.

image.altText: Crowd of people wearing face masks walking across a scramble crossing in Brisbane CBD.

items.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme

children.content: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the current blockade. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

image.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.

items.title: 'One and done': How parental burnout is affecting Australia's birth rate

children.content: Almost 60 per cent of Australian parents are experiencing psychological distress, according to the largest-ever national survey of parents as the birth rate hits a record low

image.altText: A middle aged woman with long brown hair sitting at a desk with a laptop

items.title: They’re black, white and very cute. But not all gentoo penguins are the same

children.content: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

items.title: Inside the 'worst managed infrastructure project in Australian history'

children.content: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

items.title: Trawlers may sit idle but residents are holding onto Karumba's future

children.content: Karumba's rich fishing industry dates back to the roaring, prawning era of the 60s, but major blows are seeing a seafood decline. However, the Gulf of Carpentaria residents say they "wouldn't live anywhere else".

items.title: New Qld public hospital to refuse some services for religious reasons
